title: Show HN: Deff – Side-by-side Git diff review in your terminal
summary: A Rust-based TUI for interactive git diff review with side-by-side panes, vim-like navigation, syntax highlighting, and per-file review tracking. Well-implemented developer tool but relatively niche compared to existing solutions like delta or git-delta.

title: Show HN: Hacker Smacker – Spot great HN commenters at a glance
summary: Browser extension adding friend/foe functionality to Hacker News comments. Inspired by Slashdot's system. Limited technical depth - primarily a UI/UX project with social features. Very HN-specific with minimal broader appeal.

title: Show HN: Terminal Phone – E2EE Walkie Talkie from the Command Line
summary: Single bash script providing E2EE voice and text communication over Tor hidden services. Uses Opus codec, configurable ciphers (AES/ChaCha20), and requires no infrastructure. Good technical depth covering cryptography, networking, and audio processing. Unique CLI approach to secure communication.

title: BuildKit: Docker's Hidden Gem That Can Build Almost Anything
summary: Excellent technical deep-dive into Docker BuildKit's architecture. Explains LLB (Low-Level Build definition), pluggable frontends, content-addressable caching, and how BuildKit can build artifacts beyond Docker images (APK, RPM, tarballs). High translation value for engineers wanting to understand modern build systems.

title: Show HN: Linex – A daily challenge: placing pieces on a board that fights back
summary: Browser-based puzzle game similar to Tetris with unique mechanics. Frontend JavaScript implementation. Limited technical depth - primarily an entertainment/gaming project with minimal educational or translation value for technical audiences.
